name: Fail-Soft Observability
objective: Make one governed task observable from external command through scheduling, worker execution, validation, persistence, and publication without moving authority into telemetry or exporting task content.
scope: fail-soft OpenTelemetry runtime initialization and bounded W3C traceparent propagation
closed-label Prometheus operation, duration, in-flight, failure, and operational-state metrics
content-free HTTP, MCP, scheduler, worker, validation, delivery, and PostgreSQL instrumentation
trace-correlated redacted JSON logs without raw task, output, credential, SQL, or error content
read-only operational snapshots for stuck work, leases, outbox, projection lag, approvals, and crash loops
version-pinned Prometheus, Loki, Alloy, Tempo, and Grafana development profiles
bounded dashboards, alert rules, persistent Alloy positions, and isolated clean-host rehearsal
iterative independent review, vulnerability scanning, rollback rehearsal, and two-phase closure evidence
out_of_scope: durable large-artifact storage, retention, and deletion
production authentication and remote exposure of the local observability stack
a public scheduler or MCP command for the full delivery pipeline
semantic retrieval, graph projection, and context brokering
author: codex
independent_validator: Protected GitHub Actions, CodeRabbit status, and isolated Codex CLI review of the public implementation and exact closure candidate
